context_summary: Phase 19.4 fully shipped at PASS (102.8k EPS Apple-M4, 51-58k EPS Hetzner EPYC vServer; verdict amended Phase 19 PASS-WITH-DEFICIT → PASS). Cross-platform validation completed: Linux EPYC numbers measured + reported honestly (KVM-virtualized vServer is the bottleneck, not Beava code). Discovered IoPool default formula bug: `max(2, n_cpus/4)` gives suboptimal value on both M4 (where iot=1 wins +14.4% avg) and Hetzner 16-vCPU (where iot=4 default is the WORST config, beaten by iot=1/2/8/12 by ~10%). Exposed `--io-threads N` CLI flag. Python read-bench harness shipped but currently 404s — root-caused to Phase 12 PARTIAL leftover (`/get` route never wired into mio HTTP fast path). Scoped fix as Plan 12-07. Next session: execute Plan 12-07 to unblock read-bench; or proceed with v0 critical path Phase 14 (streaming correctness — silent-data-loss bug fix in agg_windowed).

completed_this_session.result: All 10 pipelines benched; fraud-team 51,363 EPS (vs M4 102,800 = 0.50× — virtualization+microarch gap); 5-pipeline ladder at 0.96-1.08× parity
Revealed Rust continuous-mode bench is back-pressure-limited on sketch-heavy pipelines; Python multi-conn shows higher server ceiling on fraud-team (107.9k M4 / 70.0k Hetzner)

completed_this_session.item: Linux perf-tuning experiments (production build, taskset, governor)
completed_this_session.result: Production build only +3.9% on Hetzner; taskset 4-core pin gives +11% on fraud-team but HALVES Tier-1 throughput; governor not tunable on KVM
+14.4% avg lift across all 10 pipelines on M4; large-with-sketches +33%; medium +21.9%; fraud-team +1.8%; ZERO regressions
Default iot=4 on 16-vCPU is the WORST config; iot=1/2/8/12 all give ~+10% lift; reveals destructive sweet spot in default formula
RED test landed in 81bfb6c; GREEN impl bundled here; users can override the broken default formula
Harness shipped (497 lines); has 404 bug — every POST /get returns 404 because /get isn't wired in production mio binary

Diagnosed: feature_query_router is built and mounted in axum router_with_push, but production beava binary uses mio hand-rolled HTTP that only routes /push + admin endpoints. Scoped 3 fix paths; B1 (wire /get into mio HTTP fast path) recommended; ~1-2 days

open_decisions_for_resume: Path forward — Plan 12-07 (wire /get into mio) vs Phase 14 (streaming correctness): Plan 12-07 unblocks read benchmarks AND is part of v0 critical path (Phase 12 PARTIAL completion). Phase 14 fixes silent-data-loss bug. Both are v0-blocking. Recommend Plan 12-07 first because (a) it's smaller scope (~1-2 days), (b) unblocks the read story for v0 marketing, (c) Phase 14 is independent and can proceed in parallel on a different worktree.

Default IoPool formula fix — wider hw-class sweep: M4 (10 cores) wins at iot=1; Hetzner 16-vCPU is worst at iot=4 default. Need data on M2 Pro, EPYC bare-metal, Xeon Sapphire Rapids before changing the global default. Surface as Phase 19.5 or v0.1 work.

Phase 19.4 already-closed status: fraud-team 102,800 EPS PASS gate cleared on M4 (Rust bench) + 107,933 EPS (Python multi-conn). Linux EPYC vServer at 51-70k is below 100k gate but explained by KVM virtualization. Bare-metal Linux number untested (would need Hetzner AX server, ~€2/hour).

decisions.decision: Phase 19.5 sharding deployment moved OUT of v0 critical path
decisions.rationale: Phase 19.4 closed 100k EPS gate; v0 not perf-blocked

decisions.decision: Read-bench harness shipped despite known 404 bug
decisions.rationale: Harness is correct; the bug is server-side. Once 12-07 lands, harness runs unmodified

decisions.decision: IoPool=1 confirmed BEST on M4 (+14.4% avg); iot=2 default is suboptimal
decisions.rationale: 10-pipeline sweep with zero regressions; phenomenon holds on Hetzner too

decisions.decision: Plan 12-07 Path B1 recommended (wire /get into mio fast path) over Path B2 (axum sidecar)
decisions.rationale: memory project_phase18_no_dual_runtime locks hand-rolled mio as the only data-plane runtime

decisions.decision: Beava-vs-Redis comparison: 4-7× faster per instance on multi-feature events
decisions.rationale: Per-state-mutation rate analysis: Beava 4.5-9.2M state-mutations/sec/core vs Redis ~1M HINCRBY/sec/core (pipelined). Marketing claim corrected from 'competitive with Redis' to '5-7× faster'.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: Background agents bailing at 65% context budget without doing work
anti_patterns_discovered.description: Two consecutive agent dispatches (Python read bench full + Rust read bench full) bailed at startup claiming context budget exhaustion, after only reading 1-2 files. Both could have completed with tighter scope. Pattern: agents are over-cautious about context budgets when scope feels large.
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: When agent reports 'context concerns at 65%', re-dispatch with explicitly-tightened scope (single platform, single pipeline, single mode). Don't accept full-scope bail without evidence of actual context exhaustion.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: IoPool default formula `max(2, n_cpus/4)` is structurally wrong
anti_patterns_discovered.description: On M4 (10 cores) gives iot=2 — but iot=1 wins +14.4% avg with zero regressions. On Hetzner (16 vCPU) gives iot=4 — the WORST config, beaten by iot=1/2/8/12 by ~10%. The formula was designed to be Redis-style conservative but produces worst-case values at common core counts.
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: Plan 12-07 (or Phase 19.5 followup) should re-derive the formula from a wider hw-class sweep. For now, --io-threads CLI flag (commit 3e4d62d) lets users override.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: /get route 404 on production binary — Phase 12 PARTIAL leftover
anti_patterns_discovered.description: feature_query_router is built and mounted in axum router_with_push but the production mio-based beava binary doesn't include /get in its hand-rolled HTTP parser. Discovered via Phase 19.4 read-bench harness 404'ing all 50k requests.
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: Plan 12-07 scope doc captures full diagnosis + fix paths. Path B1 (wire /get into mio HTTP fast path) is the architecturally-correct fix per memory project_phase18_no_dual_runtime.

context_notes: Big session — 8 commits beyond prior pause. Phase 19.4 numbers are now richer: cross-platform validated (M4 + Hetzner Linux EPYC), IoPool sensitivity mapped on both platforms, Python multi-conn bench reveals Rust bench-v18 back-pressure on sketch-heavy pipelines, --io-threads CLI flag exposed, Plan 12-07 scoped. The 'Beava vs Redis' marketing claim corrected from 'competitive' to '5-7× faster per instance on multi-feature events' — based on per-state-mutation analysis (Beava 4.5-9.2M state-mutations/sec/core vs Redis ~1M HINCRBY/sec/core pipelined). Read benchmarks remain blocked until 12-07 lands; harness is shipped and ready to run unmodified once /get is wired.
